events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer. Need inspect image? We have image described. Need decide workable activities given H on hold indefinitely as of day 23. Activities finishing by day 23 are complete; nothing else started. Need list IDs of every activity crews can start now: not blocked by held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete.

We need analyze schedule logic.

Image schedule: Two-story addition lookahead early-start bars. Activities and predecessors:
A Mobilize & layout [2d] - (no preds? starts at day0)
B Underground rough-in [4d] - A
C Slab pour [2d] - B
CC Slab cure [3d] - C (hatched)
D Framing [7d] - CC
E Roof dry-in [5d] - D
F Electrical rough [6d] - E
G Plumbing top-out [3d] - E
H HVAC rough [4d] - E
I Rough inspection [1d] - F,G,H (red inspection hold)
J Insulation [4d] - I
K Insulation inspection [1d] - J (red)
L Drywall hang [5d] - K
M Tape & finish [4d] - L
N Paint [4d] - M
O Trim carpentry [5d] - N
P Flooring [2d] - N
Q Final inspection [1d] - O,P (red)

Disruption: Activity H (HVAC rough) is ON HOLD indefinitely as of day 23 (materials unavailable). Activities finishing by day 23 are complete; nothing else has started.

We need list activities crews can start now: not blocked by held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete.

As of day 23, activities finishing by day 23 complete. Determine which activities have finished by day 23 based on early-start bars. Need compute schedule durations/pred to identify completion days. Let's map start and finish from bar positions roughly:
A: days 0-2
B: 2-6
C: 6-8
CC: 8-11 (cure)
D: 11-18
E: 18-23
F: starts day 23? Electrical rough duration 6d, bar from ~23 to 29. It depends E. So F starts at 23, not finished by 23.
G: Plumbing top-out 3d, depends E, bar ~23-26. Not finished by 23.
H: HVAC rough 4d, depends E, bar ~23-27, on hold day 23. It hasn't finished. The hold notice says Activity H is ON HOLD as of day 23. Since nothing else started, H has not started? Wait "Activities finishing by day 23 are complete; nothing else has started." H starts day 23 per schedule. But disruption says H on hold as of day 23. So H is held, not complete. All activities whose bars finish by day 23 are complete: A,B,C,CC,D,E complete. F,G,H,I etc not started. Actually F, G, H scheduled to start at day 23. The rule says nothing else has started, so F,G,H not started (H held). Good.

Now what can start now? Need activities whose predecessors are all complete, except any predecessor held (directly or transitively) would block. Let's examine all activities:
A complete.
B complete (pred A complete) - already done.
C done.
CC done.
D done.
E done.
F: pred E complete. It does not depend on H. Can F start? It is not blocked by held H directly or transitively. Since E complete and F has no other preds. So yes, F can start now. However note Rough inspection I needs F,G,H. But F itself can start.
G: pred E complete. Does not depend on H. So G can start now. But note both F and G can proceed in parallel. Yes.
H: on hold, cannot start.
I: pred F,G,H. H held, not all preds complete, blocked transitively by H. Cannot start.
J: pred I; I blocked, so transitively blocked. Cannot start.
K: pred J; blocked.
L: pred K; blocked.
M,N,O,P,Q all downstream blocked transitively.
Also activities O Trim carpentry and P Flooring depend on N Paint, which depends on M etc. Blocked.

But wait, are there any activities with alternative paths that bypass H? No, it's a single chain after I. Only F and G are independent branches before I. Thus workable = F, G.

But need consider "not blocked by the held activity directly or transitively, with all other predecessors complete." Does starting F and G require H? No. Their predecessor E is complete. They can start now.

However, subtle: The question might consider F, G as activities that can start now because their bars start at day 23? But all activities finishing by day 23 complete; nothing else started. H hold at day 23. F and G have n…
